stem:
x²/√(x² − c²) = c²/√(x² − c²) + 39

In the given equation, c is a positive constant. Which of the following is one of the solutions to the given equation?
explanation: Subtracting c²/√(x² − c²) from both sides gives (x² − c²)/√(x² − c²) = 39, which simplifies to √(x² − c²) = 39. Squaring gives x² − c² = 39², so x² = c² + 39² and x = ±√(c² + 39²); one solution is −√(c² + 39²).
